title: C Arrays
content:
### Arrays in C

An array is a collection of elements of the same data type stored in contiguous memory locations.

### Declaration

```c
type arrayName[size];
```

### Initialization

```c
int numbers[5] = {10, 20, 30, 40, 50};
int values[] = {1, 2, 3};  // size inferred as 3
int zeros[10] = {0};       // all elements initialized to 0
```

### Accessing Elements

Array indices start at 0. The last element index is `size - 1`.

| Element | Index |
|---------|-------|
| First | `arr[0]` |
| Second | `arr[1]` |
| Last | `arr[size-1]` |

### Important Notes

- Arrays are zero-indexed
- No bounds checking (accessing out of bounds is undefined behavior)
- Array name acts as a pointer to the first element
- Arrays cannot be assigned to each other directly

---

> **Key Point:** C does not check array bounds. Accessing `arr[size]` or beyond will compile but may crash or corrupt data.

quiz.question: What is the index of the first element in a C array?
